name: CHOROID PLEXUS NEOPLASM

demographics.Incidence: 0.5-0.6% of all primary intracranial tumors; 2-5% of pediatric brain tumors; 5% of
demographics.Age: up to 20% < 1 year of age; in 75% < 2 years of age; in 86% < 5 years of age; M >> F
demographics.Path: large aggregation of choroidal fronds producing great quantities of CSF; occasionally

discriminating_features: large mass with papillary / smooth lobulated border (DDx from other intraventricular
engulfment of glomus of choroid plexus (DISTINCTIVE feature)
asymmetric diffuse ventricular dilatation = communicating hydrocephalus

general: large mass with papillary / smooth lobulated border (DDx from other intraventricular
engulfment of glomus of choroid plexus (DISTINCTIVE feature)
asymmetric diffuse ventricular dilatation = communicating hydrocephalus
dilatation of temporal horn in atrial location (obstruction)
occasionally growth into surrounding white matter (more commonly a feature of choroid
septa / cysts within ventricular system ← inflammatory reaction to tumor / tumoral
CECT: intense homogeneous enhancement ← very vascular lesion
MR: iso- to slightly hypointense lesion on T1WI + iso- to hyperintense on T2WI relative to
surrounded by hypointense signal on T1WI + hyperintense signal on T2WI (CSF)
intraventricular enhancing island of tumor on Gd-DTPA + retention of contrast within tumor
marked choline peak WITHOUT N-acetylaspartate / creatine peak; elevated lactate level
US: echogenic mass adjacent to normal choroid plexus
ANGIO: enlarged choroidal artery if neoplasm located in atrium ← supplied by anterior + posterior

clinical.Cx: (1)  Transformation into malignant choroid plexus papilloma = choroid plexus carcinoma
clinical.Rx: surgical removal (24% operative mortality) cures hydrocephalus
